feature_type: security hardening / workflow enforcement (mixed fix + feat)

checks.finding: Each stated decision has a rationale: no-new-ADR justified, the deterministic-script principle answers requirements.md Problems, pwsh choice states its basis (CI provides pwsh); guard-twin/cycle-limit choices continue the established multi-runtime pattern.
Open Questions is 'None. Owner: maintainers. Blocks Implementation: no.' with resolution path for the two prior-open decisions; all layer specs mirror the owner + non-blocking pattern.

Assumptions are grounded in requirements.md's file/line-cited evidence (sdd-hook-guard.py:1161-1164; pinned v1.0.165), describing established facts about the existing gate chain, not empirical unknowns; no investigation.md needed.

design.md ## Constraint Compliance (5 rows) covers every explicit requirements.md constraint (.ps1 ASCII, protected-not-writable, 1-issue-1-commit, no-prose-boundary, version-bump); regulatory 'none identified'; fail-closed documented.
No numeric performance requirement in requirements.md; the only related note is an acknowledged Medium matcher-latency risk with no numeric target.

## Deployment / CI Plan states the target (self-improvement.yml + 3-OS test.yml), the pipeline change, and rollback; each item is a direct always-on change via one reviewed commit, so feature-flag rollout does not apply; no schema migration.
## Data Plan states Data Entities: none, Existing Data Affected: none persisted, Migration Strategy: none -- the explicit no-data-change skip condition.

The pinned claude-code-action third-party integration underpins trust boundary B2 and is referenced in Assumptions and Risks, but design.md has no dedicated integration record naming the action's OIDC contract (claims/audience, where verified in its action.yml/release docs) or the failure behavior when the id-token:write assumption is wrong versus when the action is unavailable during a run; infra-spec/security-spec name the token but omit the action's version/contract and unavailability failure mode, leaving REQ-005 with only the circular assumption statement.
design.md Components map 1:1 onto the six REQs; no extra component/data model/endpoint; API/Contract Plan preserves every Non-goal; all Goals and User Stories covered.

High-risk surfaces (B1 write-denial, B2 CI-to-PR) each have a named validation path in ## Test Strategy (TEST-001..005/012/013, self-improvement-guard fixtures for AC-011/014, cross-model conformance for AC-008/009/016).
SKIP: no domain/ directory; CLI/CI hardening batch with no domain-model surface.
